subHeadline: A permanent, categorised reference — not symptom-chasing blog posts. Every entry links to a full breakdown of symptoms, root cause, mileage window, repair cost, and replacement cost.

rows.title: TDV6 Crankshaft Failure
rows.description: The 306DT's defining weak point — cold-start knock is the early warning. Catastrophic bearing failure, £6,500–£9,500 recon. 980 enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED].

rows.title: 276DT Oil Pump Housing Failure
rows.description: Often kills the engine outright before any other symptom appears. The 2.7 TDV6's single-point failure. 700 enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED].

rows.title: Ingenium Timing Chain Failure
rows.description: Pre-2019 204DTD tensioner wear, significantly improved from 2019 onward. £1,200–£2,500 repair cost. 1,150 enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED].

rows.title: 508PS Oil Cooler & Dilution Issues
rows.description: The 4.4 SDV8's main (and comparatively minor) weak point — oil cooler leaks can lead to coolant ingestion. 620 enquiries in 2025 [EM-VERIFIED].

rows.title: Air Suspension Compressor Failure
rows.description: The most common air suspension complaint across Range Rover and Discovery. Burnout presents a fire risk.

rows.title: Height Sensor Faults
rows.description: Trigger warning lights and uneven ride height well before a full system failure. £200–£500 repair cost.

rows.title: Valve Block & Air Spring Leaks
rows.description: Slow leaks that are often mistaken for compressor failure. £300–£800 repair cost.

rows.title: Transfer Box Faults
rows.description: Loss of drive or grinding noises on 4x4 engagement. ATC300/400 chain stretch is the most common cause.

rows.title: Differential Wear
rows.description: Front and rear diff whine, most common on high-mileage Discovery and Defender. £800–£1,500 repair.

rows.title: Propshaft & UJ Failures
rows.description: Vibration under load, a common MOT advisory across the range. £200–£500 repair.

rows.title: Terrain Response System Faults
rows.description: Sensor and module failures that disable off-road drive modes. Often linked to transfer case issues.

rows.title: Infotainment & Software Glitches
rows.description: Particularly common on pre-2018 Touch Pro systems. Can be resolved with software updates.

rows.title: Battery Drain / Parasitic Drain
rows.description: Modules failing to sleep correctly — a frequent no-start complaint on stop-start models.
